item.title: Re: why is this keter?
item.content:encoded:
My guess, it's the amount of nuisance involved- at minimum every year they have to amnestize a small town and fabricate a credible missile test on a variable date contingent on when dado gets around to calculating dado's taxes.

They also have no clue what form dado's payment will take. dado's history indicates it probably won't be something actively dangerous, but a given year's objects of significant monetary value could include anything from a loose flock of Ayam Cemani Chickens to properly packaged nuclear fuel rods.

item.title: why is this keter?
item.content:encoded: Guess this is the most harmless keter……but why keter? Since the Foundation only need to use amnestics on witness.
Godspeed, you magnificent tax-paying bastard.

item.title: Re: What a cheapskate lmao
item.content:encoded: dado good friends with uiu agents, say is for treasury dept not them problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found this due to "Recent Forum Posts" and I can't believe it took me long to figure it out. This is absolutely comedic as hell. Take your +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
one part of me asks "what exactly is the anomaly here?" another part of me shuts that part up and says "this is the funniest thing i've read this month"

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you look at the Dado hub page, he says it actually is Dado.

item.title: Peak Pedanticism
item.content:encoded: The correct way to refer to a Black Lotus would be from "Alpha" edition, not "First", edition. If you want to really go peak pedanticism, it's Limited Edition Alpha.
Congratulations Uncle Nicolini!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's pretty good as past dado objects. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Year: 2010
Object Log: …75% of a well-preserved mammoth carcass.

Year: 2014
Object Log: …25% of a moderately preserved mammoth carcass

I laughed, +1.

item.title: Re: What a cheapskate lmao
item.content:encoded: You are an insane madman, but the results are magnificent. Thank you for sharing, your additions make for a much deeper read.

item.title: Re: What a cheapskate lmao
item.content:encoded: Can I +1 a comment?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even dado fears the irs, god bless. +1

item.content:encoded: This is correct.

item.content:encoded: i noticed this too, i'm assuming it's intentional? like to say that every time he does it its different and wrong? could very well be wrong tho

item.content:encoded: dado is the correct way to spell it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dado is good friends with Edvard Munch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i trust dado. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The best part is that I absolutely believe dado has all this stuff. I cannot even try to bring myself to doubt dado.

The worst part is that 'revenue' is not actually misspelled before the (sic)???

item.title: Re: What a cheapskate lmao
item.content:encoded: This is goddamn incredible and I love it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm incredibly disappointed, Nico. +1

item.title: What a cheapskate lmao
item.content:encoded:
Alright, so me being the fool that I am, I ended up doing the math for how much he paid.

Please let me know if the math here is garbage, or if my prices are wrong! I did this in about 30 minutes of googling, so I probably made some mistakes. All prices are based on Google, so I think 2019? Hopefully.

+ 2010
- 2010

So dado paid about 81.8 mil here.

A private parking garage in Manhattan is, conservatively, about 225k.

Trying to figure out the cost of a gold coin, especially accounting for inflation, is damn near impossible, so I considered it to be a sovereign, which is worth about 1.26 USD. Adjusting for inflation of, say 2% per year (which is honestly really high for the better part of 5 centuries), and we get about 78.9 million for all those kilos, as a sovereign is about 7 grams (I think). Note: I'm not doing compounded interest for inflation here, so I'll update once I use the proper fomula.

Sable coats are about 10k each, so that's about 150k.

Now, the most complete mammoth preservation sold for about 600k, so 3/4 of that would be 475k, though I'd probably have to tack on an extra two million MINIMUM for being a whole carcass.

Edit: Alright, so turns out the formula I used for the coins was hot garbage. Instead I used the compound interest formula and got 5.2 billion dollars. Who knows.

+ 2011
- 2011

About 120 mil this year.

Honesty, the subs are fairly cheap, at least how I found them. 6 bucks a sub for 30 bucks

Scream is definitely more expensive, though, 120 mil itself at the most recent auction.

+ 2012
- 2012

Now this is a doozy. The paid amount can range from 800 mil to 6.1 billion.

Bulk of this, of course, comes from the rubies: one carat can be from 100 dollars to 15000, and with 5 carats in 1 gram, we're talking 375000 carats.

At that point, everything else is mostly irrelevant. Mickey Mantle is between 3 mil to 10 mil, Fender sells for about 1.5k itself, but I'll be generous and make it about 50k for the autograph, and the cigars are 115k per box, leading to about 575k.

Like I said, mostly irrelevant.

800 million to 6.1 billion, though I'll say at least 3 billion if not far more due to "radiant cut"

+ 2013
- 2013

Here, we see dado potentially evading taxes, paying only about 2.2 billion, though I suppose that depends on how much he paid last year as well as if his sales declined.

Landscape didn't fare well, less than 2 million based on an estimate by a museum. Y-909, on the other hand…

I did some arguing in IRC, and we concluded that a person makes roughly 20 liters of Y-909, most of which has dispersed before the Foundation could collect it. For simplicity's sake, I chose 21 for easier math. That means dado gave about 33 humans up - the equivalent of 45 million each. Black Lotus sells for about 166k each, so that's about 500k there.

+ 2014
- 2014

Alright, speculation time!

So according to Secretary Helen (all hail), Little Misters cost about 2000 each, for 40k in total…but that's without the Wondertainment signature. Based on my halfassery with the Jimi Hendrix guitar, I'll bump it up to 1.32 million.

600k for a full mammoth, so 1/4 means 125k, possibly 625k as it is preserved and not just a skeleton.

Picture of a guinea pig: obviously priceless.

+ 2015
- 2015

Ah, yes, the infamous SCP-500. How can we figure out the price of such an incredible object?

Thankfully, we do! In SCP-000-J, at least.

Based on the number of zeroes, it costs between 1 billion and 10 billion dollars; possibly more if you auction it off due to lack of market presence.

+ 2016
- 2016

Christ, that's a big statue.

Being dado, I'll assume it's the real deal: 5500kg of pure gold for 46k per kg is about 251 million.

You can't really put a price on the Holy Grail, in all honesty. For purposes for these calculations, I'll take it without religious consideration: the cost of an artifact from the 1st century could be bought for around 12k, I think. Otherwise, I'd label this as in excess of 7.5 billion.

Portrait of Rockefeller, I'd say about 400. He's not JP Morgan, he doesn't hate portraits, so they're not that special.

Voyager 1 was another 250 mil.

Goats are, at most, 300 bucks each, so we'll go with 18*300 for 5400.

Now, the recipe, though, THIS is the fun part. Estimates say the company is valued at about 188 BILLION. Here's the tricky part: you're getting the recipe, not the company itself. You might be able to blackmail yourself into owning a 188 billion dollar company, or you find yourself very much in jail for the better part of a century. On the other hand, you could bring down an entire company by leaking its secrets to the public. Overall verdict: 190 billion dollars, but only on paper.

Average key is about 7.9 grams, so we'll go with that. 40 bucks per gram, thats about 280.

Overall, this is practically worth nothing compared to the others: from only about 500 million to damn near 200 billion on paper.

2017: Now we get into the REAL tax evasion. Based on recent global events, Tojo was put up for about 3.3 million before Indonesia shut it down. 800 stocks of Amazon for about 1.2k each nets up about 960k. The motherfucker only paid 4.2 million.

Let's have UIU go after dado for tax evasion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dislike dado so, so much, and the fact that people keep managing to use him in interesting and engaging ways is aggravating on a very personal level. Upvoted, but I don't feel good about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
dado good citizen. dado pays taxes. dado respects IRS

I am not a big fan of the dado character/PoI/concept, but the idea of dado calculating the taxes and sending the pay make me smile.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, but now i really want to see responsible business capitalist person dado to interact with the Factory.

item.content:encoded: em… good shitpost…. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: beautiful article too good for this world, too pure

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
as fine capitalist, dado makes much money to buy fine art
please do not ask how dado get art, is not nice to be of asking

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is pure

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: perhaps norway museum have fake scream and only dado is of having genuine article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is the implication that dado is in possession of all these things or creates them somehow? Are the two real "The Scream"??
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll admit this for anyone to see: I'm not a fan of dado. There! I said it! A lot of the articles that feature dado as a primary character feel a bit too derpy to me. It's not that they're bad! They're just not my specific cup of tea!

That said, this article is fun, clever, and sweet in all the right ways. It might even have warmed me up to dado… but don't hold your breath.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: dado pays his taxes unlike the bezos. this makes dado better business owner.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lovely. The idea of dado sending his taxes to a random place in Washington instead of Washington D.C. is just great. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
so this article happened because djkaktus, myself, and a few others were discussing dado and our feelings towards him. kaktus and I started shitposting about dado and taxes and how it would be funny if he never paid his taxes in money or even to the IRS, and then this was born.

in case it weren't obvious, this is a co-author between myself and djkaktus.

Image is from here and is CC BY SA 4.0
